name: Halland County (Sweden)

colors.symbolism: the azure field of the Halland arms

description: A square blue flag charged centrally with a silver rampant lion, armed and langued red, on a field strewn with red hearts; the historic emblem of Halland.
history: The flag is the heraldic banner of the arms of Halland County, derived from the coat of arms of the historic province of Halland; the lion and hearts derive from the medieval Danish royal arms (Halland was a Danish province until ceded to Sweden in 1645).

features.symbolism: the azure field of the historic Halland provincial arms

a silver lion rampant, armed and langued red, on a blue field strewn with red hearts; the historic emblem of the province of Halland, derived from the Danish royal arms (Halland was Danish until 1645).
